comments.author: @RobertPerry-k5m
comments.text: Now there is no gas, soon there will be no food. The people suffer while the cowardly bunker dweller and his sycophants live in luxury as he destroys the country. Since the full-scale invasion of Ukraine in February 2022, Russia has suffered around 450,000 battlefield deaths, with total casualties—including wounded and missing—reaching 1.4 million Putin is afraid to end the war because he would have to deal with the soldiers returning from the front with memories of their fallen comrades in their minds, anger in their hearts and guns in their hands. He is afraid of a repeat of February 1917. Беспокойтесь о здоровье и безопасности вашей семьи. Генерал Зима приходит раньше, чем ты думаешь

comments.author: @TeunisMarinus
comments.text:
Comparing Hitler with Putin. 
1 Authoritarian Dictator Yes and Yes.  
2 Rigged one sided elections Yes and Yes.  
3 Surrounded by sycophants Yes and Yes.  
4 Suppress freedom of the press Yes and Yes.  
5 Destroyed free speech Yes and Yes.  
6 Secret police arbitrary arrests Yes and Yes.  
7 Kills / imprison opponents Yes and Yes.  
8 Belief in national supremacy Yes and Yes.  
9 Intolerant / homophobic Yes and Yes.  
10 Get away with seizing territory Yes and Yes. 
11 Lies about military plans Yes and Yes.  
12 Invades weaker neighbors Yes and Yes.  
13 Massacres civilian population Yes and Yes.  
14 Lies to own people Yes and Yes.  
15 Crimes against humanity Yes and Yes.  
16 Lost his war of aggression Yes and in Progress. 
17 Using Donkeys on the front Yes and Yes. 
18 Hiding in his Bunker Yes and Yes.
19 Says he has game changing weapons. Yes and Yes Hitler V1 and V2  Putin his Oreshnik missel both did nothing to change the outcome of the war. It is remarkable how much they have in common.

comments.author: @KathyWeatherman
comments.text: Beautiful City, cleaner than any large American City….Chicago, New York, San Francisco, New Orleans…just to name a few!

comments.author: @RicardoMartinez-oh9sq
comments.text: I am an American. Back in college I learned that there are different kinds of democracy. In the West it is believed that only liberal democracies are true democracies. This is not true, as most liberal democracies are corrupt political systems. The Russian Federation is a different kind of a democracy. It has a Parliament, or Duma, where even the Russian Communist Party has representatives. From the West's point of view it is an authoritarian model of democracy, just because the West's liberal model of democracy is inherently anarchistic, an oligarchic kind of anarchism. The Russian author Andre Vltchek (Russian: Андре Влчек, born December 29, 1963) explained it very well: The West, particularly Europe, is the true authoritarian political system, this is from a philosophical point of view.
